behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Rename detection depends on real `git` status/diff semantics and CLI subprocess wiring. A mocked git response can miss flag/format/version issues that cause double-counting in practice.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit `a.txt`, rename it to `b.txt` with `git mv`, run the CLI as a subprocess, parse stdout report, and assert `renamed=1`, `added=0`, `deleted=0` for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real binary diff handling is tied to subprocess output bytes/encoding and git’s binary diff behavior. In-process mocks can hide decode/parsing crashes.
behaviors.test_description: Create a repo, commit a small binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify it, run the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success, and verify the report includes the binary-file change in the correct count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real git behavior on unborn `HEAD` and command exit handling; mocks often bypass this exact edge case.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess from repo root, assert success exit code and an empty/zeroed structured report instead of an exception or stderr failure.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history and git command integration. Mocked histories can miss real command selection/range bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies, run the CLI subprocess for the target history window, and compare reported counts to expected counts derived from the known commit script.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics rely on real git index/working-tree behavior. Mocking git responses can easily hide mismatches in CLI command flags.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo with a baseline commit, edit files and run CLI once with unstaged changes, then stage the same changes and run again; assert normalized structured outputs are identical.
